LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND MOTION TO STAY JUDICAL PROCEEDINGS FILED IN LIEU OF ANSWER IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FILED A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ON JULY 14, 2016, WHICH EFFECTIVELY DISMISSED ALL OF HIS CLAIMS EXCEPT FOR THE PAGA CLAIM. ALL PARTIES AGREE THAT THE PAGA CLAIM IS NOT ARBITRABLE. IN VIEW OF THE FACT THAT PLAINTIFF INTENDS TO PROCEED ONLY ON THE PAGA CLAIM, AND HAVING FULLY CONSIDERED THIS MATTER, THE DEFENDANT'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND MOTION TO STAY JUDICAL PROCEEDINGS ARE DENIED AS MOOT. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR #6576, BUS. TEL: 530-339-5023. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Lifting Stay Of Proceedings
Off Calendar Jul-19-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Brian Conlon, Esq. (415-398-0900) of Phillips, Erlewine, Given & Carlin LLP for Plaintiff.
Patrick Gibbs, Esq. (650-843-5535) of Cooley for Defendant.

RULING - ARGUED. The Court announces the tentative ruling. Defendant argues, states the background of the case, prejudice to client. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling with addition as follows:

PLAINTIFF LENZA MCELRATH'S Motion For An Order Lifting Stay Of Proceedings is GRANTED. The stay is lifted in its entirety. This case was stayed to allow the federal action, which could have resolved this case, to proceed. The federal action is now stayed. Plaintiff demonstrates good cause to lift the stay. There will be no duplicative work or conflicting rulings given the federal stay. Plaintiff should be able to conduct discovery regarding the fraud (PAGA) claim because it is possible that key employees or board members of defendant may leave and the prejudice to defendant is minimal. Failing to lift the stay will likely lead to undue delay.

Plaintiff's Counsel has until the end of June to come up with a discovery plan and to meet and confer with Defendant's Counsel. Parties may file motions on their disagreements. Prevailing party to submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-09-2016 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Scott Erlewine and Nicholas Carlin of Phillips, Erlewine, Given & Carlin LLP telephone number 415-398-0900 for Plaintiff Lenza McElrath.
Patrick Gibbs of Cooley LLP telephone number 650-843-5535 for Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling.
Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'s demurrer to the first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to alleged violations of Labor Code 201, 202 and 204 and overruled as to the alleged violation of Labor Code 970. Mr. McElrath has not alleged sufficient facts to state a PAGA claim based on violations of Labor Code 201, 202, and 204, nor does it appear that he will be able to do, because stock options, whether vested or not, are not "wages" within the meaning of Labor Code 200(a). (IBM v. Bajorek (9th Cir. 1999) 191 F.3d. 1033, 1039.) Mr. McElrath"s PAGA notice alleges that the Labor Code 970 false representations "are contained in my Employment Agreement." The employment agreement states that the grant of stock options to Mr. McElrath is subject to the approval of Uber's Board. Liberally construing Mr. McElrath's allegations, Mr. McElrath is not contesting that the Board had the discretionary authority to disapprove his stock options grant, as long as it did so in compliance with the covenant of good faith and fair dealing implied in the employment agreement, but once the Board approved the issuance of stock options to him as it must have done, those options were to be ISOs, not NSOs. So construed, these allegations sufficiently allege a PAGA claim based on a violation of Labor Code 970. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

The Court modifies and confirms the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay Judical Proceedings Filed In Lieu Of Answer is DENIED.
